name: Deep Chocolate Cake with Rosewater Cream
recipeInstructions: Preheat oven to 275°F. Put coffee, bourbon, chocolate, and butter in a bowl over simmering water. Let chocolate melt, stirring to keep smooth. Sift flour, baking soda, and salt into another bowl. When chocolate is melted, scrape mixture into the bowl of a mixer. Add sugar slowly until dissolved. Gradually add sifted flour mixture to form a batter. Beat in eggs and vanilla until smooth. Butter a bundt or tube pan and dust with cocoa powder. Pour in batter. Bake for 1 1/2 hours, or until cake just shrinks from the sides of the pan (do not overbake). Cool completely on a rack before turning out. Dust with more cocoa powder. For serving, whip cream and flavor with rosewater. Serve a slice of cake with cream, garnished with berries and a mint sprig. You can also dust with powdered sugar. This cake keeps very well; wrap tightly in plastic wrap and store at room temperature for up to 3 days.
